behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay behavior for a gravestone object: presenting a short descriptive label when inspected and delegating complex 'use' interactions to a spawned handler.
U8P_GRAVEII::look runs on the 'U8P_GRAVEII::look' (inspect) event, composes the literal string "gravestone ", and invokes Item::bark so the inspecting actor sees/hears that label.

U8P_GRAVEII::use does minimal inline work and spawns a separate thread/script (GRAVE_NS::00CB) to perform the detailed use interaction, passing both the gravestone's pid and the caller's pid to the spawned routine.

Both handlers set per-item/process metadata (inferred set-info / process-exclude opcodes observed) and use returned values combined with an 'implies' opcode before suspending/returning; no global quest flags are read or written in this script fragment.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_GRAVEII::look is triggered by a U8P_GRAVEII::look/inspect event (Event 0).
Synchronous call: U8P_GRAVEII::look calls external Item::bark to deliver the text to the viewer, then uses the call's return value with an 'implies' opcode and suspends.
Asynchronous delegation: U8P_GRAVEII::use spawns GRAVE_NS::00CB (a separate script/thread) to handle the complex use logic, then incorporates the spawn's return via 'implies' before suspending.
Both routines perform small state/metadata writes on the item/process (inferred 'set info' and 'process exclude') prior to communication/spawn.
open_questions: What exact behavior occurs inside the spawned handler GRAVE_NS::00CB (dialog, digging, reading an epitaph, animation)? The spawn target is observed but its implementation is outside this extract.
Precise semantics of the 'set info' + 'process exclude' opcodes seen: which info field (beyond the observed constant) is being set, and whether this is a visual, state, or processing exclusion.
Exact parameter ordering expected by Item::bark in engine API (push order implies caller PID then string, but this is inferred rather than canonical).
Whether any other game-state (global flags, quest state) is modified indirectly by the spawned script (not visible in this fragment).

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVEII::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_GRAVEII::look' (inspect) action on the gravestone by producing the descriptive label "gravestone " and emitting it to the inspecting actor via Item::bark; perform small per-item metadata setup then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — invoked when an actor looks at/inspects the gravestone (the 'U8P_GRAVEII::look' verb).

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEII::look
function_map.role: Inspect (U8P_GRAVEII::look) event handler for the gravestone: builds and barks the label to the inspecting actor.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEII::use
function_map.role: Use event handler that delegates complex interaction by spawning GRAVE_NS::00CB after setting item metadata.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine used to emit a short string/message from an item/process to a target actor/process.

function_map.name: spawn GRAVE_NS::00CB
function_map.role: Spawn/launch a separate script/thread (namespace GRAVE_NS) to handle detailed 'use' interaction for the gravestone.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the literal label "gravestone " to the inspecting actor/process so the item 'speaks' or displays that text.
Delegate the detailed 'use' behavior (dialog, action, or animation) to a dedicated script/thread, passing gravestone pid and caller pid.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay behavior for a gravestone object: presenting a short descriptive label when inspected and delegating complex 'use' interactions to a spawned handler.
